 {"id":"git_safety_guard-14lu","title":"[Span] Integrate highlighting into hook denial output","description":"# Task: Integrate highlighting into hook denial output

## Parent Epic: Span Highlighting (git_safety_guard-jpfm)

## Objective

When DCG denies a command in hook mode, the stderr warning should include the highlighted match span using the formatter created in the previous task.

## Current Denial Output (src/hook.rs)

The print_colorful_warning function displays denial information. Need to enhance it to show the command with highlighted span.

## Target Output Format

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ⛔ BLOCKED by DCG                                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Command: git reset --hard HEAD                              │
│          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^                                   │
│          └── Matched destructive pattern                    │
│                                                             │
│ Pattern: core.git:reset-hard                                │
│ Reason:  Destroys uncommitted changes permanently           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

## Implementation Steps

1. Locate print_colorful_warning in src/hook.rs
2. Extract match span from EvaluationResult
3. Call highlight formatter to generate display
4. Integrate into existing warning box format
5. Test with various command lengths and match positions

## Integration Points

- src/hook.rs: print_colorful_warning function
- src/highlight.rs: format_highlighted_command (from previous task)
- EvaluationResult: source of match span data

## Acceptance Criteria

- Hook denial output shows highlighted command
- Box formatting remains clean and aligned
- Works for both short and long commands
- No performance regression (display only)

## Dependencies

- Task: Capture match byte ranges (must be complete)
- Task: Create terminal formatter (must be complete)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-14xe","title":"[PACK] cloud.aws: ECR + CloudWatch Logs destructive patterns","description":"# AWS pack gaps\n\n## Scope\nExtend cloud.aws pack to include destructive operations for ECR and CloudWatch Logs.\n\n## Destructive patterns (examples)\n- aws ecr delete-repository\n- aws ecr batch-delete-image\n- aws ecr delete-lifecycle-policy\n- aws logs delete-log-group\n- aws logs delete-log-stream\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n- [ ] Patterns added with reasons + tests\n- [ ] Keywords updated if needed (ecr, logs)\n- [ ] E2E pack cases added","notes":"Implemented in src/packs/cloud/aws.rs: added keywords ecr/logs and destructive patterns for ecr delete-repository/batch-delete-image/delete-lifecycle-policy and logs delete-log-group/delete-log-stream; added unit test coverage.\n\nQuality gates: cargo fmt --check failed due to pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated files; cargo check --all-targets passed with warnings; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings failed due to pre-existing clippy issues in src/packs/test_helpers.rs and src/evaluator.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T16:59:40.636186362-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:04.071766261-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:04.071766261-05:00","close_reason":"Patterns + tests added; gates blocked by unrelated pre-existing format/clippy issues"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-16f","title":"[CATEGORY] loadbalancer.* - Load Balancer Pack Suite","description":"# Load Balancer Pack Suite

## Overview
Load balancers control traffic routing. Misconfiguration can cause outages or route traffic incorrectly. Backend removal drops active connections.

## Packs to Implement

### loadbalancer.nginx
- **Why Critical**: nginx is the most popular reverse proxy. Config changes require reload and can cause downtime.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `nginx -s stop` - Stops nginx entirely
  - `systemctl stop nginx`
  - Editing nginx.conf with upstream removal
  - Deleting files in /etc/nginx/ configs
- **Safe Patterns**: `nginx -t` (config test), `nginx -T` (dump config)

### loadbalancer.haproxy
- **Why Critical**: HAProxy handles high-traffic load balancing. Backend changes affect live traffic.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `systemctl stop haproxy`
  - Config changes removing backends
  - `haproxy -f` with bad config
- **Safe Patterns**: `haproxy -c` (config check)

### loadbalancer.elb
- **Why Critical**: AWS ELB/ALB/NLB handle cloud traffic routing. Target group changes affect service availability.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer`
  - `aws elbv2 delete-target-group`
  - `aws elbv2 deregister-targets`
  - `aws elb delete-load-balancer` (classic)
- **Safe Patterns**: `describe-`, `list-`

### loadbalancer.traefik
- **Why Critical**: Traefik is popular in container environments. Dynamic config changes affect routing immediately.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - Container/pod deletion affecting Traefik
  - Config removal from dynamic config directory
- **Safe Patterns**: `/api/`, `/dashboard/` endpoints

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 2 Epic (git_safety_guard-hhh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major load balancer tools covered
- [ ] Config file operations detected
- [ ] Service stop/restart operations handled
- [ ] Cloud provider LB APIs covered","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:28:55.05887554-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:29:40.61994914-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:29:40.61994914-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-0rf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:46.101876708-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:46.131800716-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-tff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:57.329681659-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-eoj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:57.36474505-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.651826103-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.697069589-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.743970184-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.79018607-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.836981135-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.885231549-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri","title":"Rule-Level Metrics and Analytics","description":"# Epic: Rule-Level Metrics and Analytics

## Priority Score: 550/1000 (Fifth Priority)

## Background and Rationale

Users and maintainers need to understand which patterns are effective and which are noisy. This enables data-driven pattern tuning and builds confidence that DCG is providing value.

Currently DCG tracks history but does not aggregate by rule_id for analytics. This epic adds rule-level metrics showing hit counts, allowlist override rates, and effectiveness scores.

## Example Output

dcg stats --rules

Shows:
  Rule                        Hits   Allowed   Noise%   Last Hit
  ----------------------------------------------------------------
  git.reset-hard               47        3      6.4%    2h ago
  filesystem.chmod-777         23       18     78.3%    1d ago  [NOISY]
  docker.system-prune          12        0      0.0%    3h ago
  
  Recommendations:
  - Consider reviewing filesystem.chmod-777 (78% allowlist rate)
  - git.reset-hard is working effectively (low override rate)

## Technical Approach

1. Ensure rule_id is captured in every history entry
2. Add analytics queries grouped by rule_id
3. Calculate metrics: hit count, allowlist rate, time since last hit
4. Identify noisy patterns (high allowlist rate)
5. Generate recommendations for tuning

## Metrics Calculated

- Total hits per rule
- Allowlist override count and percentage
- Time since last trigger
- Trend (increasing/decreasing over time)
- Effectiveness score (hits - allowlist overrides)

## Key Files

- src/history/schema.rs - Ensure rule_id field populated
- src/history/mod.rs - Add analytics query methods
- src/stats.rs - Rule-level aggregation
- src/cli.rs - dcg stats --rules command

## Success Criteria

- Every denial logs rule_id in history
- Stats view shows per-rule breakdown
- Noisy patterns flagged automatically
- Recommendations actionable and clear

## Dependencies

This epic provides foundation for Auto-Suggested Allowlists epic, which needs rule_id tracking to cluster commands effectively.

## Note on Existing Implementation

DCG already has some pattern effectiveness tracking (PatternEffectiveness, PackEffectivenessAnalysis). This epic extends and surfaces that functionality in a user-friendly CLI view.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:05:06.219714293-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:05:06.219714293-05:00"}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.1","title":"[Metrics] Reuse existing history structs (PatternEffectiveness, etc.)","description":"# Task: Reuse existing history structs (PatternEffectiveness, etc.)

## Purpose
Avoid duplicate analytics paths by explicitly reusing existing history types
(e.g., PatternEffectiveness, PackEffectivenessAnalysis, PatternStat).

## Requirements
- Inventory current history analytics structs
- Decide which ones to reuse vs extend
- Document mapping from new rule-metrics needs to existing types

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rule-metrics design references existing structs
- No duplicate or competing analytics pipelines introduced
","notes":"## Inventory of Existing Analytics Structs

### Core Structs for Reuse

1. **PatternEffectiveness** (schema.rs:2402)
   - Fields: pattern, pack_id, total_triggers, denied_count, bypassed_count, bypass_rate
   - Reuse: Extend with optional `rule_id` field for stable pattern identification
   - This is the primary struct for per-rule analytics

2. **PatternStat** (schema.rs:248)
   - Fields: name, count, pack_id
   - Reuse: Simple summary counts; can be grouped by rule_id in queries
   - Used for top-N pattern lists

3. **HistoryStats** (schema.rs:325)
   - Fields: period_days, total_commands, outcomes, block_rate, top_patterns, etc.
   - Reuse: Wrapper for aggregated stats; top_patterns uses PatternStat
   - No changes needed; queries can group by rule_id internally

4. **PackEffectivenessAnalysis** (schema.rs:2471)
   - Fields: high_value_patterns, potentially_aggressive (both Vec<PatternEffectiveness>)
   - Reuse: Already uses PatternEffectiveness; benefits from rule_id extension

## Mapping to Rule-Level Metrics

| New Metric Need | Existing Struct | Approach |
|-----------------|-----------------|----------|
| Per-rule trigger counts | PatternEffectiveness | Add rule_id field |
| Per-rule bypass rates | PatternEffectiveness | Already has bypass_rate |
| Rule effectiveness trends | PatternEffectiveness + timestamps | Query by rule_id with time grouping |
| Top rules by volume | PatternStat | Query groups by rule_id |

## Recommended Changes

1. Add optional `rule_id: Option<String>` to PatternEffectiveness
2. Update effectiveness queries to use rule_id for grouping when available
3. Keep backward compat: fall back to pack_id:pattern_name if rule_id is NULL

## No New Structs Required
The existing PatternEffectiveness struct covers rule-level metrics needs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:21:18.574744451-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:21:42.094279024-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:21:42.094279024-05:00","close_reason":"Design complete: documented existing structs (PatternEffectiveness, PatternStat, HistoryStats, PackEffectivenessAnalysis) and mapping to rule-level metrics needs. PatternEffectiveness is the primary struct to extend with rule_id field.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:21:18.57611411-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.2","title":"[Metrics] CLI output format + JSON option for rule metrics","description":"# Task: CLI output format + JSON option for rule metrics

## Purpose
Provide both human-readable and machine-readable output for rule-level metrics.

## Requirements
- Pretty table output in terminal
- JSON output suitable for CI or dashboards
- Stable field names for rule_id, pack_id, counts, allowlist rate

## Acceptance Criteria
- `dcg stats --rules --format json` produces valid JSON
- Pretty output includes totals and top N rules

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.5","title":"[Metrics] Unit tests for rule_id capture in history","description":"# Task: Unit tests for rule_id capture in history

## Purpose
Guarantee that rule_id is reliably recorded for all deny/warn outcomes in history.

## Requirements
- Unit tests that exercise at least one core rule and verify stored rule_id
- Tests for allowlist override cases (ensure original rule_id recorded)

## Acceptance Criteria
- History entries include correct rule_id

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.6","title":"[Metrics] Time-series trending and historical analysis","description":"# Task: Time-Series Trending

## Background
Track how rule hit patterns change over time to identify trends.

## Implementation
- Store timestamped hit counts in history DB
- Calculate week-over-week changes
- Identify increasing/decreasing trends
- Flag anomalies (sudden spikes)

## CLI Output
```
dcg stats --rules --trend

Rule                  Hits (7d)  Trend    Change
git.force-push           12      ↑ +50%   (8 last week)
filesystem.rm-rf          5      ↓ -40%   (8 last week)
docker.prune              3      → stable
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Trending data calculated correctly
- Anomaly detection for spikes > 200%
- JSON output includes trend data","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:38:03.273895806-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T15:38:03.273895806-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:38:03.27533686-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1g6","title":"Replace evaluator mock tests with real pack fixtures","description":"## Objective

Eliminate mock pattern structs in `src/evaluator.rs` tests by exercising the real pack registry + pattern types.

## Scope

- Identify mock types (`MockSafePattern`, `MockDestructivePattern`) and replace with real `packs::SafePattern`/`packs::DestructivePattern`.
- Use minimal real packs or a small test-only pack registry with real regexes.
- Ensure tests verify decision outputs and match metadata (pack_id/pattern_name/reason).

## Notes

- Avoid fakes: use actual `REGISTRY` where practical, or create a real `Pack` instance (not a mock) with real regexes.
- Keep performance-sensitive behavior (quick reject, normalization) covered via real inputs.

## Acceptance criteria

- No `Mock*Pattern` types remain in evaluator tests.
- Coverage of evaluator decisions stays or improves.
- Tests remain deterministic and do not depend on external state.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","title":"Performance budgets + benchmark enforcement","description":"## Objective

Codify performance budgets and enforce them in CI so `dcg` stays invisible.

## Budgets to encode

- Fast path (no heredoc/inline): p99 under 500us
- Slow path (heredoc/inline): p99 under 20ms
- Absolute max: fail-open beyond 50ms

These numbers can be revised, but must be explicit.

## Requirements

- Benchmarks for:
  - quick reject / keyword gating
  - normalization
  - pack evaluation
  - overrides/allowlist
  - heredoc trigger/extract/AST path
- A CI job that compares benchmarks to budgets (tolerant of variance).

## Acceptance criteria

- Budgets exist as code constants/config.
- CI can detect large regressions and fail.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.4","title":"CI workflows: core suite vs deep suite (fuzz/proptest/bench)","description":"## Objective

Add CI workflows that run the right tests at the right cadence.

## Core suite (per PR)

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
- selected E2E script coverage

## Deep suite (scheduled or manual)

- proptest ignored tests
- fuzzing time-limited runs
- benchmarks with budget checks

## Acceptance criteria

- CI is fast enough for PR iteration.
- Deep suite can be run without human babysitting.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.5","title":"Regression corpus harness (true/false positives + bypass attempts)","description":"## Objective

Create and maintain a small, curated corpus of high-signal command cases and a harness that runs them through:

- hook JSON path
- CLI evaluator path

## Corpus categories

- `true_positives/` (must block)
- `false_positives/` (must allow)
- `bypass_attempts/` (must block or warn)
- `edge_cases/` (must not crash/hang)

## Output/logging requirements

- Harness prints per-case:
  - description
  - expected decision
  - actual decision
  - rule id when matched

## Acceptance criteria

- Corpus harness runs in CI.
- Adding a new regression is easy and does not require writing Rust test code each time.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-7ug8","title":"[Metrics] Implement dcg stats --rules CLI view","description":"# Task: Implement dcg stats --rules CLI view

## Parent Epic: Rule-Level Metrics (git_safety_guard-1dri)

## Objective

Create a CLI view that displays per-rule metrics in a clear, actionable format.

## Command Interface

dcg stats --rules [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --since <DURATION>     Time period (default: 30d)
  --sort <FIELD>         Sort by: hits, noise, last (default: hits)
  --limit <N>            Max rules to show (default: 20)
  --format <FMT>         Output format: table, json (default: table)
  --noisy                Only show rules with >50% override rate

## Table Output

$ dcg stats --rules --since 30d

Rule Metrics (last 30 days)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Rule ID                       Hits    Overrides   Noise%   Trend   Last Hit
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
core.git:reset-hard           127          3       2.4%     ↗      2h ago
core.filesystem:chmod-777      89         71      79.8%     ↘      1d ago  ⚠
containers.docker:prune        45          2       4.4%     →      6h ago
core.git:push-force            38          5      13.2%     ↗      30m ago
kubernetes.kubectl:delete      23          8      34.8%     →      3d ago

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Summary: 5 rules shown, 283 total denials, 15.4% average override rate

⚠ Recommendations:
  • core.filesystem:chmod-777 has 79.8% override rate - consider reviewing
  • core.git:reset-hard is trending up - may indicate workflow issue

## Symbols

- ↗ Increasing trend
- → Stable
- ↘ Decreasing trend
- ⚠ Noisy (>50% override)

## JSON Output

For scripting and dashboards:

{
  "period": {"since": "2024-01-01", "until": "2024-01-31"},
  "rules": [
    {
      "rule_id": "core.git:reset-hard",
      "hits": 127,
      "overrides": 3,
      "override_rate": 0.024,
      "trend": "increasing",
      "last_hit": "2024-01-31T10:30:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_denials": 283,
    "avg_override_rate": 0.154
  },
  "recommendations": [...]
}

## Recommendations Engine

Generate actionable recommendations:

fn generate_recommendations(metrics: &[RuleMetrics]) -> Vec<Recommendation> {
    let mut recs = Vec::new();
    
    for m in metrics {
        if m.override_rate > 0.5 {
            recs.push(Recommendation::ReviewNoisyRule(m.rule_id.clone()));
        }
        if m.trend == Trend::Increasing && m.total_hits > 50 {
            recs.push(Recommendation::InvestigateTrend(m.rule_id.clone()));
        }
    }
    
    recs
}

## Key Files

- src/cli.rs - Add stats --rules subcommand
- src/stats.rs - Rule metrics formatting

## Acceptance Criteria

- Table output is clear and aligned
- JSON output is valid and complete
- Recommendations are actionable
- Sorting and filtering work
- Handles empty data gracefully

## Dependencies

- Task: Analytics queries

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","title":"[PACK] apigateway.kong - Kong API Gateway Pack","description":"# Kong API Gateway Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Kong Gateway CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### kong CLI
- `kong delete services`
- `kong delete routes`
- `kong delete plugins`
- `kong delete consumers`
- `kong delete upstreams`
- `kong delete targets`

### deck CLI (declarative config)
- `deck reset` - DANGEROUS! Removes all config
- `deck sync --select-tag X` (with destructive intent)

### Admin API
- `curl -X DELETE :8001/services/`
- `curl -X DELETE :8001/routes/`

## Safe Patterns
- `kong get services`
- `kong list routes`
- `deck dump`
- `deck diff`
- `deck ping`
- GET requests to Admin API

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: apigateway.* (git_safety_guard-5qa)
- Keywords: kong, deck
- CRITICAL: `deck reset` is extremely dangerous

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for service/route deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for `deck reset` detection
- [ ] Unit tests for Admin API patterns
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/apigateway_kong.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Service/route deletion blocked
- [ ] `deck reset` blocked
- [ ] Read operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:40.90288906-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:51:21.96479239-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:51:21.96479239-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Kong API Gateway pack with 10 destructive patterns, 13 safe patterns, and 13 tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.721392863-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.767496377-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.811952462-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.855625033-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.900828574-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.947308937-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.899030399-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.949776003-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7zg","title":"Add UBS installation and run steps to CI workflow","description":"# Task: Add UBS Installation and Run Steps to CI

## What to Do

Add two steps to the check job in .github/workflows/ci.yml:
1. Install UBS using the official installer
2. Run UBS on changed Rust files

## CI Steps to Add

After the clippy step, add:

```yaml
- name: Install UBS
  run: |
    curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner/master/install.sh" \
      | bash -s -- --easy-mode
    # Add UBS to PATH for this job
    echo "$HOME/.ubs" >> $GITHUB_PATH

- name: Run UBS on changed files
  if: always()
  run: |
    echo "=== UBS Static Analysis ===" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    echo "Timestamp: $(date -Iseconds)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    
    # Get changed Rust files
    if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
      CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD -- "*.rs" | tr "\n" " ")
      echo "Mode: Pull Request (base: ${{ github.base_ref }})" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    else
      CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1 -- "*.rs" | tr "\n" " ")
      echo "Mode: Push (comparing to HEAD~1)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    fi
    
    echo "Changed files: ${CHANGED:-none}" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    
    if [ -n "$CHANGED" ]; then
      echo "Running UBS analysis..." | tee -a ubs-output.log
      # Run UBS with verbose output
      if ubs --verbose $CHANGED 2>&1 | tee -a ubs-output.log; then
        echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
        echo "UBS Result: PASSED (no issues found)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
      else
        echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
        echo "UBS Result: WARNINGS (issues found - non-blocking)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
        echo "::warning::UBS found potential issues in changed files"
      fi
    else
      echo "UBS Result: SKIPPED (no Rust files changed)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    fi
    
    # Summary
    echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    echo "=== End UBS Analysis ===" | tee -a ubs-output.log

- name: UBS summary
  if: always()
  run: |
    echo "## UBS Static Analysis" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
    echo "\`\`\`" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
    cat ubs-output.log >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY 2>/dev/null || echo "No UBS output" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
    echo "\`\`\`" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

- name: Upload UBS output
  if: failure()
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: ubs-output
    path: ubs-output.log
    retention-days: 7
```

## Key Fixes from Original

1. **Binary Path**: Added `echo "$HOME/.ubs" >> $GITHUB_PATH` to make ubs available
2. **Detailed Logging**: All output goes to ubs-output.log with timestamps
3. **Clear Status**: Explicit PASSED/WARNINGS/SKIPPED messages
4. **Artifact Upload**: Save output on failure for debugging
5. **Step Summary**: UBS results appear in GitHub Actions summary

## Why --verbose

The verbose flag provides:
- File-by-file analysis progress
- Detailed issue descriptions
- Context around found issues

## Why Warning Mode

Using warning annotations instead of failing because:
- UBS is new to this repo, may have false positives
- Dont want to block PRs unexpectedly
- Can change to blocking after tuning

## Files to Modify

- .github/workflows/ci.yml (check job)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] UBS install step added with PATH setup
- [ ] UBS run step added with detailed logging
- [ ] Changed-files logic works for PRs and pushes
- [ ] ubs-output.log captures all analysis output
- [ ] Step summary shows UBS results
- [ ] Artifacts uploaded on failure
- [ ] CI passes with new steps","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- CI must capture UBS install/run logs and upload them as artifacts on failure.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-85p","title":"[PACK] monitoring.prometheus - Prometheus/Grafana Pack","description":"# Prometheus/Grafana Monitoring Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Prometheus and Grafana operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Prometheus
- Deleting rule files in rules.d/
- `promtool` operations that modify rules
- Deleting recording rules
- TSDB admin API: `curl -X POST .../api/v1/admin/tsdb/delete_series`

### Grafana
- `grafana-cli plugins uninstall`
- API: `curl -X DELETE .../api/dashboards/`
- API: `curl -X DELETE .../api/datasources/`
- API: `curl -X DELETE .../api/alert-notifications/`

### Kubernetes
- `kubectl delete` on monitoring namespace
- Deleting ServiceMonitor/PodMonitor CRDs
- Deleting PrometheusRule CRDs

## Safe Patterns
- `promtool check rules`
- `promtool query`
- `curl` GET to Prometheus API
- Grafana dashboard viewing

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: monitoring.* (git_safety_guard-adw)
- Keywords: promtool, grafana-cli, prometheus
- Note: Often deployed in Kubernetes

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for rule file deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for Grafana API patterns
- [ ] Unit tests for k8s CRD deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/monitoring_prometheus.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Rule deletion blocked
- [ ] Dashboard/datasource deletion blocked
- [ ] Query operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:10:29.192244637-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:59:10.20626104-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:59:10.20626104-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-85p","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.084053527-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-85p","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.124662093-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-85p","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.166736817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-85p","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.208554017-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-85p","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.251076173-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-85p","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.291471327-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-85p","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.498175565-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-85p","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.544419803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
